intro: Colubrina tepicana, also known as Tepicana or Mexican Silverberry, is a small shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, thanks to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Colubrina tepicana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Colubrina tepicana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical applications for wound healing and skin issues.
